chapter_title.ar: باب الصَّلَاةِ خَلْفَ مَنْ يُقْتَدَى بِهِ وَ الْقِرَاءَةِ خَلْفَهُ وَ ضَمَانِهِ الصَّلَاةَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 53 – The Salāt behind the one who is followed by, and the recitation behind him, and his responsibility of the Salāt

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/263)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/263)
text: إِذَا كُنْتَ خَلْفَ إِمَامٍ تَرْتَضِي بِهِ فِي صَلَاةٍ يُجْهَرُ فِيهَا بِالْقِرَاءَةِ فَلَمْ تَسْمَعْ قِرَاءَتَهُ فَاقْرَأْ أَنْتَ لِنَفْسِكَ وَ إِنْ كُنْتَ تَسْمَعُ الْهَمْهَمَةَ فَلَا تَقْرَأْ .

en.hubeali: And from him, from his father, from Abdullah Bin Al Mugheira, from Quteyba,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘When you were behind a Imam you are pleased with in a Salāt wherein is loud recitation, but you cannot hear his recitation, so recite to yourself; but if you can hear the humming (of the Imam), so do not recite’.
en.sarwar: 4. It is a narration from him (narrator of previous Hadith) by his father from ‘Abd Allah ibn al-Mughirah from Qutaybah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has said, ‘When you perform Salah (prayer) behind an Imam and are happy with him in Salah (prayer) of which recitation is to be read aloud and you do not hear his recitation, then recite to yourself, but if you hear a humming sound, do not recite.’”
